steps.title: Find the vertex and determine if it is a maximum or minimum
steps.content: The equation is in vertex form $$y = a(x-h)^2 + k$$ with $$a = -16$$, $$h = 6.9$$, and $$k = 762$$. Thus the vertex is $$(h,k) = (6.9,762)$$. Because $$a < 0$$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex represents the maximum height. Hence the maximum height is $$k = 762$$ feet and it occurs at time $$x = h = 6.9$$ seconds.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex coordinates as time and maximum height
steps.content: In this context, $$x$$ measures time in seconds after launch and $$y$$ measures height in feet. Therefore the firework reaches its maximum height of 762 feet at 6.9 seconds after launch. That matches choice C — C is correct.
